metadata.title: Testing Business Ideas — 44 Experiment Card Library
metadata.scope: Canonical experiment card reference for Step 8 (PDSA Experiment Designer) and cross-referenced by precoil-emt (Phase 3) and cxif-bmi-coach (Phase 5 Part C)
metadata.methodology: Testing Business Ideas by David J. Bland and Alexander Osterwalder
metadata.quality_note: Card names normalized from consultant-friendly version. Duplicates from the original source (Search Trends at row 37/44, Ad Campaign at row 7/45) have been resolved to exactly 44 unique entries.

sequencing_rules: Move from cheap evidence to expensive evidence
Move from simulated value to real value
Move from first use to repeat use

experiments.name: Desk Research
experiments.what_it_tests: Existing market facts, customer context, baseline assumptions
experiments.best_used_when: You need a fast baseline before fieldwork

experiments.name: Search Trends
experiments.what_it_tests: Interest over time, demand signals, language people use
experiments.best_used_when: You need directional interest evidence

experiments.name: Keyword Research
experiments.what_it_tests: Search intent, problem awareness, demand phrasing
experiments.best_used_when: You need search-intent evidence

experiments.name: Patent Search
experiments.what_it_tests: Technical novelty, solution space, IP barriers
experiments.best_used_when: You need to understand IP or technical space

experiments.name: Competitor Analysis
experiments.what_it_tests: Alternatives, market structure, positioning pressure
experiments.best_used_when: You need baseline market structure understanding

experiments.name: Analogous Markets
experiments.what_it_tests: Comparable models, adoption patterns, transferability
experiments.best_used_when: You want to infer patterns from similar spaces

experiments.name: Expert Interviews
experiments.what_it_tests: Industry constraints, technical or regulatory insight
experiments.best_used_when: You need specialist input on technical constraints

experiments.name: Problem Interviews
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether the problem is real, frequent, painful
experiments.best_used_when: You need to confirm whether a problem is real and painful

experiments.name: Solution Interviews
experiments.what_it_tests: Initial reaction to a proposed solution
experiments.best_used_when: You want early reaction to a concept

experiments.name: Customer Observation
experiments.what_it_tests: Real behavior in context, workarounds, unmet needs
experiments.best_used_when: You need real behavior, not opinions

experiments.name: Ethnographic Field Study
experiments.what_it_tests: Deep context, latent jobs, environmental constraints
experiments.best_used_when: Behavior is complex and embedded in context

experiments.name: Contextual Inquiry
experiments.what_it_tests: Workflow details, edge cases, actual task execution
experiments.best_used_when: You need to understand task execution in context

experiments.name: Diary Study
experiments.what_it_tests: Repeated behaviors, recurring pains, time-based patterns
experiments.best_used_when: The behavior unfolds over time

experiments.name: Journey Mapping
experiments.what_it_tests: Friction points, handoffs, service gaps
experiments.best_used_when: You need a clear view of end-to-end friction

experiments.name: Focus Groups
experiments.what_it_tests: Group reactions, language, themes, objections
experiments.best_used_when: You want rapid group reaction and language

experiments.name: Surveys / Questionnaires
experiments.what_it_tests: Pattern detection at scale, stated preferences
experiments.best_used_when: You need pattern detection at scale

experiments.name: Forced Ranking
experiments.what_it_tests: Priority among features, pains, gains, alternatives
experiments.best_used_when: You need prioritization across options

experiments.name: Card Sorting
experiments.what_it_tests: Mental models, classification logic, usability structure
experiments.best_used_when: You need to understand mental models

experiments.name: Storyboard
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether users understand and relate to the scenario
experiments.best_used_when: You need to test comprehension of a scenario

experiments.name: Paper Prototype
experiments.what_it_tests: Early usability and workflow assumptions
experiments.best_used_when: You want to test a flow cheaply

experiments.name: Wireframe Prototype
experiments.what_it_tests: Interaction logic, navigation, flow clarity
experiments.best_used_when: You need better fidelity without building

experiments.name: Throwaway Prototype
experiments.what_it_tests: Functional assumptions without building the real thing
experiments.best_used_when: You want to simulate functionality quickly

experiments.name: 3D Prototype
experiments.what_it_tests: Physical interaction, ergonomics, form-factor assumptions
experiments.best_used_when: Physical form matters

experiments.name: Usability Testing
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether users can successfully use the solution
experiments.best_used_when: You want to know if users can complete tasks

experiments.name: Explainer Video
experiments.what_it_tests: Comprehension, resonance, initial interest
experiments.best_used_when: You need to test whether people understand the idea

experiments.name: Ad Campaign
experiments.what_it_tests: Click-through interest, audience targeting, message pull
experiments.best_used_when: You want quick paid traffic evidence

experiments.name: Landing Page
experiments.what_it_tests: Interest, sign-up intent, value proposition pull
experiments.best_used_when: You want behavioral evidence of interest

experiments.name: Fake Door
experiments.what_it_tests: Behavioral interest in a feature or offer before building
experiments.best_used_when: You want to test interest before building

experiments.name: Mock Sale
experiments.what_it_tests: Real purchase intent before full delivery exists
experiments.best_used_when: You want to test commercial intent before delivery

experiments.name: Pre-Order Test
experiments.what_it_tests: Commitment to buy before the full product exists
experiments.best_used_when: You need real commitment before build

experiments.name: Letter of Intent
experiments.what_it_tests: Commercial seriousness, buying intent, stakeholder alignment
experiments.best_used_when: You need a stronger buying signal in B2B

experiments.name: Crowdfunding
experiments.what_it_tests: Market pull plus willingness to pay in public
experiments.best_used_when: You want public proof of demand and payment

experiments.name: Concierge Test
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether the value can be delivered manually before automating
experiments.best_used_when: You want to manually deliver value before automating

experiments.name: Wizard of Oz
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether users value the output before the backend is built
experiments.best_used_when: You want users to experience automation before building it

experiments.name: Single-Feature MVP
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether one core capability creates enough value
experiments.best_used_when: One feature is the core bet

experiments.name: Piecemeal MVP
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether the offer works using stitched-together tools/manual ops
experiments.best_used_when: You can stitch existing tools together

experiments.name: Minimum Viable Product
experiments.what_it_tests: End-to-end usage, value capture, operational viability
experiments.best_used_when: You need real usage evidence

experiments.name: Paid Pilot
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether customers will pay for real-world trial usage
experiments.best_used_when: You want real usage with payment

experiments.name: Presales
experiments.what_it_tests: Actual commercial commitment before full scale-up
experiments.best_used_when: You want actual sales conversations and commitments

experiments.name: Price Testing
experiments.what_it_tests: Price sensitivity, acceptable range, pricing objections
experiments.best_used_when: Pricing is uncertain

experiments.name: Revenue Model Test
experiments.what_it_tests: Subscription vs usage vs license vs service logic
experiments.best_used_when: Business model is uncertain

experiments.name: Channel Test
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether acquisition/distribution channels actually work
experiments.best_used_when: Go-to-market route is uncertain

experiments.name: A/B Testing
experiments.what_it_tests: Message, offer, design, or flow optimization
experiments.best_used_when: You are optimizing a live message or flow

experiments.name: Cohort / Retention Analysis
experiments.what_it_tests: Whether value persists and users keep coming back
experiments.best_used_when: You need evidence users stay and return

branches.question: Do customers really have this problem or want this?

sub_branches.context: You know very little about the customer/problem
You want to test reaction to a solution or message
You want behavioral evidence, not opinions

branches.question: Can we actually build and deliver this?

sub_branches.context: You want to test usability or flow cheaply
You want to simulate the experience before building
You want to test physical interaction
You want to test end-to-end delivery

branches.question: Will people pay, and can this become a business?

sub_branches.context: You want to test willingness to pay early
You want to test how money will be made
You want to test how the offer reaches customers
You want to test whether value persists over time

branches.question: You need background evidence before any field test

quick_selection_rules.question: Can this scale as a business?

step_8_behavior: Use only the 44 canonical experiment card names defined in this file — never invent new ones.
Match cards to assumptions by DVF category first, then by evidence strength appropriate to current confidence level.
Recommend evidence strength progression: Weak → Medium → Strong. Never recommend Strong evidence experiments when no prior evidence exists.
Use the sequencing transitions (usually_runs_after, usually_runs_next) to recommend experiment order.
Use the decision tree to help users select experiments when they describe what they're unsure about.
When recommending cards for precoil-emt Phase 3, include 1-3 cards with evidence strength ratings and one sentence on why each fits the assumption.

cross_agent_integration.precoil_emt_phase_3: After generating an experiment brief, recommend 1-3 specific cards from this library matched to the assumption category and evidence level.
cross_agent_integration.cxif_bmi_coach_phase_5c: When designing PDSA experiments from canvas-derived assumptions, recommend specific cards matched to the DVF category of each assumption.
